feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Fiori
feed.subtitle: SAP Fiori Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Previewing Fiori Standard Extension Project on BAS
entry.content:
Do anyone know how can I preview an Extension Project on SAP Business Application Studio ?
I manage to preview other custom Fiori Apps, but cant preview any of the Extension Projects of Standard Fiori Projects, such as Look Up Retail Products.
Is there a version prerequisite or any steps that was missing ?

Many Thanks.

entry.title: SAPUI5 Treetable client-side Binding without any ODATA SERVICE/Backend by using mockserver
entry.content:
Happy new year fellas!✨🎉
So I was playing around with the Treetable in SAPUI5, I had no backend-API/ODataservice to get the data from to bind my table with. Then i decided to go with the MockServer(Middleware)

 


We will navigate through the following steps :

STEP 1 : Creating a project in SAP BAS/VSCode IDE using metadata document - 

**NOTE - If you are using VSCode, you can directly enter the path of the metadata.xml ,and if you are using SAP BAS, you have to upload the metadata.xml file to your projects folder before going for project creation.(I did the same😅) ,sharing my metadata.xml if you in case you wanna use mine.

 

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" standalone="yes"?>
<edmx:Edmx Version="1.0"
xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2008/09/edm"
xmlns:edmx="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/06/edmx"
xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata"
xmlns:edmanno="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2009/02/edm/annotation"
xmlns:sap="http://www.sap.com/Protocols/SAPData">

<edmx:DataServices m:DataServiceVersion="2.0">
    <Schema Namespace="DemoModel" xml:lang="en" sap:schema-version="1">
        
        <EntityType Name="Node">
            <Key>
                <PropertyRef Name="NodeID" />
            </Key>
            <Property Name="NodeID" Type="Edm.Int32" Nullable="false" edmanno:StoreGeneratedPattern="Identity" sap:hierarchy-node-for="NodeID"/>
            <Property Name="HierarchyLevel" Type="Edm.Int32" Nullable="false" sap:hierarchy-level-for="NodeID"/>
            <Property Name="Description" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="false" MaxLength="40" Unicode="true" FixedLength="false" />
            <Property Name="ParentNodeID" Type="Edm.Int32" Nullable="true" sap:hierarchy-parent-node-for="NodeID"/>
            <Property Name="DrillState" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="false" MaxLength="10" Unicode="true" FixedLength="false" sap:hierarchy-drill-state-for="NodeID" />
        </EntityType>
        
        <EntityContainer Name="DemoEntities" edmanno:LazyLoadingEnabled="true" m:IsDefaultEntityContainer="true">
            <EntitySet Name="Nodes" EntityType="DemoModel.Node" />
        </EntityContainer>
        
    </Schema>
</edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>

 

 

STEP 2: Check the dependency that the mock server is installed.

And Also in ui5-mock.yaml if your mockdataPath is there is not.

**NOTE - If you need to install mock server manually to a project, see the following.

Install MockServer From Here 

STEP 3: Create your json file having your mock data in "webapp/localService/data/your_filename.json". my json(Nodes.json) is given below.

 

[
    {
        "NodeID": 1,
        "HierarchyLevel": 0,
        "Description": "Company",
        "ParentNodeID": null,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 2,
        "HierarchyLevel": 0,
        "Description": "Departments",
        "ParentNodeID": null,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 3,
        "HierarchyLevel": 0,
        "Description": "Projects",
        "ParentNodeID": null,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 4,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "HR",
        "ParentNodeID": 1,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 5,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "Finance",
        "ParentNodeID": 1,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 6,
        "HierarchyLevel": 2,
        "Description": "Payroll",
        "ParentNodeID": 5,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 7,
        "HierarchyLevel": 2,
        "Description": "Budgeting",
        "ParentNodeID": 5,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 8,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "IT",
        "ParentNodeID": 2,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 9,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "Marketing",
        "ParentNodeID": 2,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 10,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "Sales",
        "ParentNodeID": 2,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 11,
        "HierarchyLevel": 1,
        "Description": "Project A",
        "ParentNodeID": 3,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 12,
        "HierarchyLevel": 2,
        "Description": "Phase 1",
        "ParentNodeID": 11,
        "DrillState": "expanded"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 13,
        "HierarchyLevel": 3,
        "Description": "Task 1",
        "ParentNodeID": 12,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 14,
        "HierarchyLevel": 3,
        "Description": "Task 2",
        "ParentNodeID": 12,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 15,
        "HierarchyLevel": 3,
        "Description": "Task 3",
        "ParentNodeID": 12,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    },
    {
        "NodeID": 16,
        "HierarchyLevel": 3,
        "Description": "Task 4",
        "ParentNodeID": 12,
        "DrillState": "leaf"
    }
]

 

STEP 4 : XML View with TreeTable Control. (If you are using Fiori Elements , the process is same upto here, and you should get the preview with hierachical data in table after step 3, just make sure your metadata is having the hierarchy oData annotations like my metadata, else it wont work😓).

 

<mvc:View controllerName="ztreetable.controller.View1"
    xmlns="sap.ui.table"
    xmlns:m="sap.m"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc">
    <m:Page id="page" >
        <TreeTable
            id="treeTable"
            selectionMode="Single"
            enableColumnReordering="false"
            rows="{
                path : '/Nodes',
                parameters : {
                    countMode: 'Inline',
                    numberOfExpandedLevels: 1
                }
            }">

            <columns>

                <Column>
                    <m:Label text="Description"/>
                    <template>
                        <m:Link text="{Description}" wrapping="false" />
                    </template>
                </Column>

                <Column>
                    <m:Label text="HierarchyLevel"/>
                    <template>
                        <m:Text text="{HierarchyLevel}" wrapping="false" />
                    </template>
                </Column>

                <Column>
                    <m:Label text="NodeID"/>
                    <template>
                        <m:Text text="{NodeID}" wrapping="false" />
                    </template>
                </Column>

                <Column>
                    <m:Label text="ParentNodeID"/>
                    <template>
                        <m:Text text="{ParentNodeID}" wrapping="false" />
                    </template>
                </Column>

            </columns>

        </TreeTable>
    </m:Page>
</mvc:View>

 

STEP 5 : Preview your app using npm run start-mock 😎 (DONT USE NORMAL PREVIEW SCRIPT).
**NOTE - Make sure all your paths are correct and specially the name of the json file should be same as the pathname of "rows" attribute of TreeTable Control.

entry.title: Fiori Elements Developer Extensibility Doubt
entry.content:
Hello Community
I am trying to adapt a screen of the ES5 demo Gateway through 'SAP Business Studio'. My question is how can I open the 'SAP UI5 Visual Editor' to add new fields to the screen?When I right-click on the 'manifest', the 'Open SAP UI5 Visual Editor' option is not displayed (see attachment 1). I tried the 'Open Adaptation Editor' option but nothing happens.

entry.title: Issue with hasdraftentity in sap cap odata v4
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I have worked on list report based on SAP CAP, when i select any line item i get below error, Any suggestions are appreciated

Error : Failed to drill-down into (ID=48243eff-54a4-4e0d-b7b6-5f11057bc663,IsActiveEntity=true)/HasDraftEntity, invalid segment: HasDraftEntity - monitor/Invoices?$count=true

Error : Failed to drill-down into (ID=48243eff-54a4-4e0d-b7b6-5f11057bc663,IsActiveEntity=true)/DraftAdministrativeData, invalid segment: DraftAdministrativeData - monitor/Invoices?$count=true&$orderby=IssueDate%20desc

entry.title: Deployment Error : 403 error in Fiori App deployed in S4Hana Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP-ians,

I am currently working on an integration project between SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and a External API. 
I am facing an issue with my Fiori app deployed on the S/4HANA Public Cloud. The app is working fine locally, but when deployed on the cloud, I am encountering a 403 Forbidden error when attempting to access backend data.

The solution involves the following steps:

  1. CDS and CAP Application:

    • I have created a Core Data Services (CDS) view in public cloud and consumed it in SAP BTP Business Application Studio (BAS).
    • I have developed a CAP application where I consumed the CDS OData service and implemented the backend logic for posting data to the External API.

  2. Deployment to SAP BTP:

    • The CAP backend is deployed to Cloud Foundry in SAP BTP, and I have created a destination for the deployed BAS service URL, which I am using to connect the Fiori app to the backend.
    • I have also tested my CAP backend service in Postman it is working fine.

       


      In Cap Destination I have also tried using the Authentication type as OAuth2clientCredentials.

  3. Fiori App Development:

    • I created a Fiori app that consumes the backend data from the CAP application. (the Fiori and CAPM are two different Projects in BAS).
    • The app works fine when running locally in the SAP BTP BAS environment. I'm able to interact with the external API through CAP backend as expected.

       

  4. Deployment to S/4HANA Public Cloud:

    • I successfully deployed the Fiori app as a Fiori tile to the S/4HANA Public Cloud.
    • In public Cloud, While accessing the deployed Application, the UI of the app loads correctly, I am facing a 403 error when trying to access the backend data (while loading the metadata of the backend).

       

  5. IAM and Authorization:

    • While Deployment, I have created the IAM App in ADT for the app, but since I am using a CAP backend (not RAP), I am unsure of how to bind the service of CAP to the Application to resolve the 403 error. Typically, the RAP service is bound to the app to resolve such issues, but since I am not using RAP, I need guidance on how to handle this scenario. I didn't added any services to this APP.

       

    • Additionally, I see a UAA roles is created in BTP environment up on deployment of CAPM application, if I assign the role to user will it resolve this 403 error.

       

My Questions:

  • What could be causing the 403 error when accessing the CAP backend from the deployed Fiori app on S/4HANA Public Cloud? is it due to lack of authorization or roles in BTP?
  • Are there specific configurations or steps I need to follow when using a CAP backend instead of RAP for Fiori apps deployed on S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  • Is there any option available to import the CAP service to ABAP environment so that I could add the CAP service to application in IAM?
  • How can I resolve the 403 error and ensure that the Fiori app can access the backend data?
  • Will the error resolve if I assign the UAA roles to the respective User in BTP?

Any insights or guidance would be greatly appreciated!

Many Thanks in Advance!


Best Regards,
Arasu

SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA ,SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: MTB build failure after upgraded to npm 11
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We had a MTA project was deployed on BTP cloud foundry,

Initially, we are using node v18, npm 10 without issue.

From current release, BTP required our project to use NPM v11, so that we upgraded all related node modules. And project running on local service without issue.

But when we tried to run the 'Mbt build' to build the mta project (mbt version: v1.2.34),  we are facing following error,

and we checked the "boast" files, both of them are found in respective location.

[2025-01-05 18:02:52]  INFO the build results of the "raasapp2-srv" module will be packaged and saved in the "/Users/i3****/Documents/*****/working/raasdata_project/raasdashboard/raasdatatst4/.raasdatatst4_mta_build_tmp/raasapp2-srv" folder
[2025-01-05 18:03:01] ERROR could not package the "raasapp2-srv" module when archiving: could not read the "/Users/i3****/Documents/****/working/raasdata_project/raasdashboard/raasdatatst4/node_modules/.bin/boast" symbolic link: stat /Users/i3****/Documents/****/working/raasdata_project/raasdashboard/raasdatatst4/node_modules/swagger2openapi/boast.js: no such file or directory
make: *** [raasapp2-srv] Error 1
[2025-01-05 18:03:01] ERROR could not build the MTA project: could not execute the "make -f Makefile_20250105180232.mta p=cf mtar= strict=true mode=" command: exit status 2
Error: could not build the MTA project: could not execute the "make -f Makefile_20250105180232.mta p=cf mtar= strict=true mode=" command: exit status 2

 following are the dependencies in package.json

"engines": {
    "node": "^20",
    "npm": "11.0.0"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "4": "^0.0.0",
    "6": "0.0.1",
    "@cap-js-community/odata-v2-adapter": "^1.13.8",
    "@sap_oss/alert-notification-client": "^1.7.0",
    "@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client": "^3.25.0",
    "@sap-cloud-sdk/mail-client": "^3.18.1",
    "@sap/audit-logging": "^5.8.1",
    "@sap/cds": "^8.6.0",
    "@sap/cds-compiler": "^5.6.0",
    "@sap/cds-dk": "^8.6.1",
    "@sap/hdbext": "^8.1.0",
    "@sap/logging": "^6",
    "@sap/textbundle": "^4.3.0",
    "@sap/xsenv": "^5.4.0",
    "@sap/xssec": "^4.2.7",
    "axios": "^1.7.9",
    "cap-llm-plugin": "^1.4.5",
    "cds-swagger-ui-express": "^0.10.0",
    "cheerio": "^1.0.0-rc.12",
    "cors": "^2.8.5",
    "csv-parse": "^5.5.5",
    "express": "^4",
    "fast-csv": "^4.3.6",
    "generic-pool": "^3.9.0",
    "hana-cli": "^3.202405.1",
    "hdb": "^0.19.8",
    "passport": "^0.6.0",
    "pdfkit": "^0.13.0",
    "pdfkit-table": "^0.1.99",
    "request": "^2.88.2",
    "sap-hdbext-promisfied": "^2.202410.1",
    "uuid": "^11.0.3"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@cap-js/cds-typer": "^0.31.0",
    "@sap/eslint-plugin-ui5-jsdocs": "^2.0.8",
    "@sap/ux-specification": "^1.124.7",
    "@sapui5/ts-types": "^1.92.2",
    "chai": "^4.3.10",
    "chai-as-promised": "^7.1.1",
    "chai-subset": "^1.6.0",
    "eslint": "^9.17.0",
    "jest": "^29.7.0"
  },

following is the mta.yaml

_schema-version: "3.1"
ID: raasapp2
description: RaaS CAP project.
version: 1.0.22
modules:
- name: raasapp2-srv
  type: nodejs
  path: .
  requires:
  - name: raasapp2-db
  - name: uaa_raasapp2
  - name: raas_email_srv
  provides:
  - name: srv-api
    properties:
      srv-url: ${default-url}
  parameters:
    buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
    disk-quota: 2048M
    memory: 2048M
    stack: cflinuxfs4
  build-parameters:
    #builder: npm-ci
    ignore:
    - '*default-env.json*'
    - ./db/*.env*
    #- ./db/node_modules
    - ./app
    #- ./app/node_modules
    - ./mta_archives
    - ./db/ddl
    - ./db/dml
    - .git
    - .*.DS_Store
    - .*/*/.DS_Store
    - .*/*/*/.DS_Store


- name: raasapp2-db-deployer
  type: hdb
  path: db
  requires:
  - name: raasapp2-db
    properties:
      TARGET_CONTAINER: ~{hdi-service-name}
  # - name: cross-container-service-1
  #   group: SERVICE_REPLACEMENTS
  #   properties:
  #     key: ServiceName_1
  #     service: ~{the-service-name}
  parameters:
    buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
    stack: cflinuxfs4
  build-parameters:
    ignore:
    - default-env.json
    - .env
    #- '*node_modules*'


- name: app
  type: approuter.nodejs
  path: app
  requires:
  - name: uaa_raasapp2
  - name: srv-api
    group: destinations
    properties:
      forwardAuthToken: true
      name: srv-api
      url: ~{srv-url}
  parameters:
    disk-quota: 256M
    memory: 256M
    keep-existing-routes: true
    stack: cflinuxfs4
  build-parameters:
    ignore:
    - default-env.json
    - .env
    #- '*node_modules*'

resources:
- name: raasapp2-db
  type: com.sap.xs.hdi-container
  parameters:
    service: hana
    service-plan: hdi-shared
  properties:
    hdi-service-name: ${service-name}
# - name: cross-container-service-1
#   type: org.cloudfoundry.existing-service
#   parameters:
#     service-name: CC_ACCESS
#   properties:
#     the-service-name: ${service-name}
- name: uaa_raasapp2
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    path: ./xs-security.json
    service: xsuaa
    service-name: raasapp2-xsuaa-service
    service-plan: application
- name: raas_email_srv
  type: org.cloudfoundry.existing-service
  parameters:
    service-name: raas_email_service
  properties:
    the-service-name: ${service-name}
parameters:
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
  - builder: custom
    commands:
    #- npm config set registry https://registry.npmjs.com/
    #- npm ci
    - npx -p @SAP/cds-dk@latest cds build --production

can anyone give some clue to fix the problem?

entry.title: SAP UI5 Control for input with floating label
entry.content:
In one of our Ui5 apps in SAP BTP ,
1.we have a requirement to have a view with input fields having floating label which is shown in the attachment .When we searched the SAPUI5 SDK for the availability of this control , unfortunately we could not find one.
So here we are requesting the SAP team to guide us if there is any SAPui5 control available which we can use for the input Field with Floating label.
2.and for the Document upload is there any control which exactly matches the one which is there below in the  attached document.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: is it possible to create a dual column bubble chart using viztype as combination
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am trying to create a dual column (or) dual bar with bubble chart using viztype as combination but it was not recognizing the bubble chart it will take only line and bar using the datashape in plot area how to do.

entry.title: SAP Fiori based Table Maintenance Object "DEVC does not exist"
entry.content:
This is a question already asked in 2023 (Solved: SAP Fiori based Table Maintenance Error on Public ... - SAP Community), but I can't add a comment, the question seems to be locked, so I'm posting the question again along with an answer.

Steps to reproduce:

  1. Select a customizing table
  2. Select "Generate Other Repository Objects"
  3. Select "Maintenance Object"
  4. There's an error "DEVC does not exist"

My system: ABAP 7.58 SP 0.

entry.title: Intent-based Navigation Parameters
entry.content:
In an Overview Page, I have annotations for Intent-based navigation to another app. Can someone please tell me how to send parameters to the target app?

I've been scouring SAP documentation and everywhere else trying to find out how we're supposed to send parameters, but cannot find anything! Even looking at SAP's standard ovp pages, I can't find any annotation they've used to send specific parameters to target app.

I have a simple table card:

<Annotation Term="UI.LineItem" Qualifier="TableCardLI">
                    <Collection>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataField">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="accounting_office"/>
                        </Record>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataFieldForAnnotation">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Target" AnnotationPath="@UI.DataPoint#AmtDP"/>
                        </Record>
                    </Collection>
                </Annotation>
                <Annotation Term="UI.Identification">
                    <Collection>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataFieldForIntentBasedNavigation">
                            <PropertyValue Property="SemanticObject" String="I2PCheckIn"/>
                            <PropertyValue Property="Action" String="deeplink"/>
                            <PropertyValue Property="Label" String="navigate"/>
                        </Record>
                    </Collection>
                </Annotation>

When it navigates to target app, it sends a whole bunch of parameters, except the field that I want to send!

I tried to set "Accept additional parameters" in the target mapping to Ignore, Allowed or Disallowed. It makes no difference.

Can anyone point me to an example or documentation that explains how parameters are sent through intent-based navigation in FE?

Thank you.

entry.title: Apps not available in FRUN launchpad
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

We are newly installing FRUN system. When loading FRUN FLP for the first time, I could not find any of the apps available. 

Could you please let me know what configuration step could be missing. I tried following most of the SAP blogs but could not get into the issue.

FYI, I am following FRUN_Masterguide_403.pdf  for FRUN configuration.

One of the note I found to be related 3368272 - Fiori Launchpad shows no tiles when accessed using protocol HTTP or using protocol HTTPS through SAP Web Dispatcher - SAP for Me,
But I am already using standard logon procedure.

Regards,

Priyanshu

entry.title: Confirm Warehouse Task call failing from a custom SAP FIORI app to S/4HANA Public Cloud API
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am trying to call action ConfirmWarehouseTaskExact for a existing open Warehouse task in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud via following code :

const sPathAct = `/WarehouseTask(EWMWarehouse='Z10',WarehouseTask='1000000232',WarehouseTaskItem='0')/com.sap.gateway.srvd_a2x.api_warehouse_order_task_2.v0001.ConfirmWarehouseTaskExact(...)`
const oAction = oModel.bindContext(sPathAct
     ,undefined  //null
     ,{
         "DirectWhseTaskConfIsAllowed": true

      }

                            ); 

     // Execute the action
oAction.invoke(undefined,true).then( function(oResult) {
   MessageToast.show("Warehouse task confirmed successfully.");
   }).catch(function(oError) {
      MessageToast.show(oError.toString());
   });    

and I am facing following error:

Error: Not a bound action: /WarehouseTask(EWMWarehouse='Z10',WarehouseTask='1000000232',WarehouseTaskItem='0')/com.sap.gateway.srvd_a2x.api_warehouse_order_task_2.v0001.ConfirmWarehouseTaskExact(...)

    at l.createCacheAndRequest (ODataContextBinding-dbg.js:650:10)

    at ODataContextBinding-dbg.js:256:17

    at SyncPromise-dbg.js:314:14

    at e (SyncPromise-dbg.js:63:4)

    at new r (SyncPromise-dbg.js:230:3)

    at r.then (SyncPromise-dbg.js:313:7)

    at l._invoke (ODataContextBinding-dbg.js:236:5)

    at l.invoke (ODataContextBinding-dbg.js:1413:15)

    at c.onMovePallet (MovePalletsNewBinToPallet.controller.js:203:33)               


I double checked and  the following entity does exist in the S4H backend system:
/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_warehouse_order_task_2/srvd_a2x/sap/warehouseorder/0001/WarehouseTask(EWMWarehouse='Z10',WarehouseTask='1000000232',WarehouseTaskItem='0')

What could be wrong with my code ?

Thanks!

Br
Mikko

entry.title: Key Figure calculation, formula to check for ONLY values (NOT NULL) in attribute on Planning Object.
entry.content:
I am trying to configure a keyfigure with checks such as: 

Condition 1: At customer product and shipto level, XYZPROP key figure has 0 value in for CURRENT TO 13 PERIODS- for which I created an helper key figure. with IBP_DYNAMIC_RAGGR("ABC@MTHPRODLOCCUSTWE",''SUM'', 0, 13, ''CURRENTFUTURE'')  and Condition2: If attribute SHIPFROMDEFAULTLOC at customer product loc and shipto level is NOT blank, then check the SHIPFROMDEFAULTLOC value is equal to locid then assign ZWEPROPSTAT with the ZWEADJUSTEDACTUALSQTY in the future buckets to that level record.

What formula in the key figure calculation would work to check for planning objects that has values (NOT NULL) in attribute on Planning Object? Per my understanding, we can only use static value. Is that true?

entry.title: not added to the banking hierarchy
entry.content:
Hi, all

I am trying to register a bank account in Fiori. When I attempt to save, the message "It is not added to the banking hierarchy" appears. When I check in GUI using FI12, the account is indeed not displayed. The bank itself is registered. How can I resolve this issue?

entry.title: SAP Fiori Rich Text Editor - content visible only when Edit is clicked.
entry.content:
It is not possible to see content in standard mode for “Overview”, whereas when “Edit” is clicked then we can see content.

Rich Text Area is blank, in Standard mode: 

Rich Text Area content is visible, in Edit mode:

Any help would be gratefully appreciated, thanks in advance.

entry.title: sap.m.SplitApp linking within the JSON file
entry.content:
We have an application, a split app - sap.m.SplitApp

The data for the split app is held in a local data.json file.  Which some contains some HTML which has all been generated and showing as needed.

We have a need to link from the data in the JSON file to a page ID in the split app.

For Example -

In the data.json file we have

"page1Intro": "<p> If your looking for page 2 <Link text="Click Here" press="onPressNavToPage1"/> </p>"

Please note the " have been converted to "

in the View.xml

<Label text="Introduction" id="labelheaSTD003"/>
<FormattedText htmlText="{policyModel>/0/page1Intro}" width="100%" id="inputheaSTD002"/>

When viewing its showing

Introduction    If your looking for page 2 

The link is not working.

Also tried <a href=#page2/><strong>Page 2</strong></a> but the HREF will only link to a DIV ID not a page ID, is it possible to add a DIV ID to a Split App?

entry.title: Git: /bin/bash: warning: setlocale: LC_ALL: cannot change locale (en_US.UTF-8)
entry.content:
When trying to commit my Fiori project to Git from SAP Business Application Studio, I'm getting the following error:

"Git: /bin/bash: warning: setlocale: LC_ALL: cannot change locale (en_US.UTF-8)"

Any idea how I get round this?

entry.title: What to consider when selling self-developed Fiori applications?
entry.content:
I am an experienced full stack developer working as an employee with large S/4HANA systems. I developed an fiori app (including a RAP backend) that i want to sell to different companies. It will run on S/4HANA On-Premise and Private Cloud. I don´t want to use the SAP Store but instead promote it via my network and sell it directly.

What do i need to consider when doing so?

As far as I understand it, selling a self-built Fiori app is not really different from selling development services. In both cases, SAP technologies (SAPUI5, RAP, ABAP, ...) are being used and the application always runs on customer systems which are licensed SAP systems.

I already got in contact with the SAP Partner Support, but they weren´t able to give me a clear answer. They only gave information regarding the partner program, but not if it is actually necessary in my case or what options i have as a one-person company.

entry.title: How to define the "data-sap-ui-resource-roots" of OPA qunit test for MTA project (Fiori freestyle)
entry.content:
Hi,

We are building a testing modules for existing full stack SAP UI5 project, our project structure looks like this..

project
-app
--node_modules
--webapp
---myAppInst
----controller
----views
----resources(my question is here)
----test
------Test.qunit.js
----manifest
---xs-app.json
---package.json
-db
-srv
-xs-security.json
-node_modules
-package.json

i found SAPUI5 and OPENUI5 is using different resources folder, like the sap/ui/test modules,  when i tried to install the modules, files were created in my node_modules folder, but when i running the Test.qunit.html, it is always referring to the resources folder. Therefore, to make it work, i copied the openUI module file from node_modules to /webapp/myAppInst/resource folder. 

here is my Test.qunit.html

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
	
	<!-- src="../../../../node_modules/@openui5/sap.ui.core/src/sap/ui/test/starter/runTest.js" -->
	<meta charset="utf-8">
	<script
	src="../resources/sap/ui/test/starter/runTest.js"
		data-sap-ui-resource-roots='{
			"test-resources.myAppInst": "./"
		}'
	></script>
</head>
<body class="sapUiBody">
	<div id="qunit"></div>
	<div id="qunit-fixture"></div>
</body>
</html>

But after that, the qunit test journey having following error, looks like the qunit test is looking up all the UI5 modules from my resources folder, instead the node_modules folder

Unable to load the component with the name: myAppInst
Failure in Opa check function
Exception thrown by the testcode:'Error: Error while waiting for promise scheduled on flow, details: ModuleError: failed to load 'sap/m/library.js' from ../resources/sap/m/library.js: script load error
ModuleError: failed to load 'sap/m/library.js' from ../resources/sap/m/library.js: script load error
    at we (http://localhost:5001/myAppInst/webapp/resources/ui5loader.js:6:6833)
    at ge.failWith (http://localhost:5001/myAppInst/webapp/resources/ui5loader.js:6:4709)
    at HTMLScriptElement.i (http://localhost:5001/myAppInst/webapp/resources/ui5loader.js:6:10686)
Caused by: Error: script load error
    at HTMLScriptElement.i (http://localhost:5001/myAppInst/webapp/resources/ui5loader.js:6:10728)
Error: Error while waiting for promise scheduled on flow, details: ModuleError: failed to load 'sap/m/library.js' from ../resources/sap/m/library.js: script load error
ModuleError: failed to load 'sap/m/library.js' from ../resources/sap/m/library.js: script load error
    at we (http://localhost:5001/myAppInst/webapp/resources/ui5loader.js:6:6833)
    at ge.failWith (http://localhost:5001/myAppInst/webapp/resources/ui5loader.js:6:4709)
    at HTMLScriptElement.i (http://localhost:5001/myAppInst/webapp/resources/ui5loader.js:6:10686)
Caused by: Error: script load error
    at HTMLScriptElement.i (http://localhost:5001/myAppInst/webapp/resources/ui5loader.js:6:10728)
    at oOptions.check (http://localhost:5001/myAppInst/webapp/resources/sap/ui/test/Opa.js:677:12)
    at Opa5._executeCheck (http://localhost:5001/myAppInst/webapp/resources/sap/ui/test/Opa5.js:1278:26)
    at oOptionsPassedToOpa.check (http://localhost:5001/myAppInst/webapp/resources/sap/ui/test/Opa5.js:820:18)
    at fnClass.<anonymous> (http://localhost:5001/myAppInst/webapp/resources/sap/ui/test/Opa.js:591:37)
    at opaCheck (http://localhost:5001/myAppInst/webapp/resources/sap/ui/test/Opa.js:54:18)
    at wrappedCallback (http://localhost:5001/myAppInst/webapp/resources/sap/ui/test/autowaiter/_timeoutWaiter.js:120:17)'

can anyone give some suggestion?

entry.title: BAS deploy UI5 Application
entry.content:
Hi,

I am using BAS to deploy a own developed SAP Fiori App to a backend system. (ECC + gateway in one system)

Deployment goes well, even if there are errors in the logs. (so cannot really complain about that)

but... when i change something in the frontend and want to redeploy i get a success messages saying its ok i can see the changes in the transport have run all refresh programs as your suppost to nut the changes do not take effect. i have also tried to undeploy and it gets "deleted". deploying under same name just give me the old version (that was suposibly deleted) even though depolyment was a success.

The only way to get the new app to deploy with the new content is to deploy under a different name. 

this is really not a solution 😕

has anyone had the same problem who found out what i am doing wrong?

It is a normal Fiori freestyle APP not using elements

br Lisa
